The same data for Ti50 as in the
Gamma rays were registered by a pair gamma
spectrometer consisting of Ge(Li) detector (63 cm3)
surrounded by a four-section NaI(Tl) circular detector.
The spectrometer was placed at 55 degrees to the beam.
The scattering chamber was fixed rigidly in a lead
protection (55 m) of the detector unit.
The error of the partial cross sections
obtained from the spectra is actually determined by
the experimental error (~20%), which is made up of:
the error in calibration of the absolute
efficiency of the pair gamma spectrometer (15%),
the error in determination of the target
the error in determination of the charge
obtained by electrolytic deoisition onto a tantalum
was fabricated by electrolytic precipitation on a
300 mu-m thick tantalum backing. The target was
oriented with respect to the proton beam at 55 degrees.
